channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates into Prolonged Strategic Confrontation
item.description: The article discusses the evolving conflict between the US and Iran, highlighting how their confrontation has transitioned from diplomatic efforts to a prolonged cycle of military and strategic engage...

item.title: Canadian Mathematician Jacob Tsimerman Wins Prestigious Fields Medal
item.description: Jacob Tsimerman, a 38-year-old professor at the University of Toronto, has been awarded the Fields Medal, mathematics' highest honor, alongside three U.S. scholars. This marks the first time a Canadia...

item.title: Enugu Air Plane Skidded Off Runway At Benin Airport; All Evacuated Safely
item.description: An aircraft operated by Enugu Air veered off the runway after landing at Benin Airport, triggering an emergency response. The airline confirmed all passengers and crew were safely evacuated with no in...

item.title: African Marketplace Dubai 2026: A Global Platform for African and Caribbean Entrepreneurs
item.description: African Marketplace (AMP) is set to return for its second edition from October 10–12, 2026, at the Conrad Hotel Dubai. This event aims to connect African and Caribbean entrepreneurs with global buyers...

item.title: Government Pauses Early Prison Release Scheme Amid Victim Concerns
item.description: The UK government has paused a controversial early prison release scheme following widespread criticism from victims' families and survivors. Justice Secretary Alex Norris announced the decision after...

item.title: US Coast Guard Aids in Rescue of Injured Filipino Navyman Amid Tensions with China
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed that a US Coast Guard vessel assisted in the medical evacuation of Filipino soldiers injured during an encounter with China Coast Guard (CCG) personnel at A...

item.title: Two Cordillera municipalities enforce pork and hog entry ban to combat ASF threat
item.description: Two municipalities in Mountain Province, Barlig and Bontoc, have implemented a ban on the entry and transport of live hogs, pork, and pork products to safeguard the local swine industry from African s...

item.title: Parliamentary committee faces delays as report on criminal justice inquiry faces criticism
item.description: Members of parliament's ad hoc committee investigating corruption and political interference in the criminal justice system continue to face challenges in finalizing their report. The committee met to...

item.title: US-Saudi Nuclear Deal Sparks Concerns Over Middle East Proliferation Risks
item.description: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear cooperation agreement, allowing Saudi Arabia to build reactors using American technology and enrich uranium. While the deal aims to strengthen ec...

item.title: Measles Outbreaks and Vaccination Challenges in the United States
item.description: The article details the resurgence of measles in the United States, highlighting the challenges posed by vaccine hesitancy and misinformation. It describes how unvaccinated individuals are contributin...

item.title: Western Cape Launches New Psychiatric Unit to Enhance Mental Health Care
item.description: The Western Cape Department of Health and Wellness has officially opened a new 30-bed Acute Psychiatric Unit (APU) at Eerste River Hospital, marking a significant step in expanding mental health infra...

item.title: Controversy over Canada-US bridge deal and Prime Minister's transparency
item.description: The article critiques Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's handling of the Gordie Howe International Bridge revenue-sharing agreement with the United States. It highlights the discrepancy between public st...

item.title: Philippines Rejects China's Claim on Sierra Madre Construction, Clarifies Legal Stance
item.description: Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro denied China's claim that construction activities on the BRP Sierra Madre violated a provisional agreement. During a press briefing, Lazaro emphasized that...

item.title: Release of Presidential Funds to Federal Information Agencies Delayed Amid Dispute Over Approval Process
item.description: Funds approved by President Bola Tinubu for major federal information agencies have not been released following a disagreement over the approval process within the Federal Ministry of Information and ...

item.title: Smart heating systems cut power bills by half with energy-efficient technology
item.description: A smart-home heating system automatically adjusts to temperature, humidity, and occupancy, slashing energy costs by up to 50%. Portable heaters are the most expensive option, but reverse-cycle systems...

item.title: Journalist injured by pellet guns during Delhi student protests
item.description: A journalist from Outlook magazine sustained pellet wounds while covering student protests in Delhi, according to medical records from Safdarjung Hospital. The injuries, described as 26 small round wo...

item.title: Critical dialogue on child health and safety
item.description: The article highlights the importance of open and honest conversations between parents and children to ensure their safety and well-being. It emphasizes that discussing topics like stranger danger, em...

item.title: US Considers Escalating Military Actions Against Iran Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has indicated he is nearing a decision to escalate military actions against Iran, with the US military planning strikes 'bigger than ever before' as tensions in the Middle East ...

item.title: Nigeria Targets 20 Gold Medals at 2026 Commonwealth Games
item.description: Nigeria is set to make a bold statement at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, aiming for 20 gold medals after a dismal showing in their first participation in 1950. The team, consisting of 72 ath...

item.title: Border Patrol Agent Indicted for Excessive Force After Striking Non-Threatening Suspect
item.description: A Border Patrol agent, Raymundo Ramos Jr., was indicted for allegedly accelerating his vehicle into a suspect who was walking calmly and posed no threat to officers. The incident occurred on January 3...
